title: Replace an assertion with a concrete comparable set
guidance: Reward named, relevant comparables linked back to the claim. Penalise generic examples.

runner_input.instruction: Support the assertion below with a comparable set from assurance-driven industries, not adjectives.
runner_input.constraints: 3–5 named analogues. For each: did AI compress pricing or not, and why. Then one line linking back.

source_blocks.text: Assertion to support: 'AI will not compress Halden's pricing, because customers pay for assurance, not for the schedule artefact itself.' Context: in some assurance markets AI has held pricing (work is paid for as an outcome / a vouching service); in others where the artefact is the product and accuracy is checkable, AI has compressed pricing.

rubric_notes: Strong comps: law (assurance held), accounting (shifted to advisory), tax advice (held), legal/court transcription (held, regulatory requirement), medical transcription (collapsed — artefact, checkable).
The link back: Halden holds only if the purchase is assurance, not a checkable artefact.
